source: CAS Academic Policies §Withdrawing from and Dropping Courses (line 514)
body:
**CAS withdrawal windows depend on when you drop:**

- **First two weeks of the term:** the course is dropped and does NOT appear on your transcript. The CAS bulletin says: *"Courses dropped during the first two weeks of the term will not appear on the transcript."*
- **Beginning of the third week through the 14th week:** the course is recorded with a grade of **W**. *"Those dropped from the beginning of the third week through the 14th week of the term will be recorded with a grade of W. (This grade is not calculated in the GPA and indicates a student withdrew in good academic standing.)"*
- **After the 14th week:** *"After the 14th week, no student may withdraw from a course unless there are extenuating circumstances"* and a petition is approved by the College's Committee on Academic Standards (25 West 4th Street, 6th floor; 212-998-8140).

If you're ill or have a serious personal issue, contact the College Advising Center first (25 West 4th Street, 5th floor; 212-998-8130) to discuss a late-withdrawal petition.

F-1 students: dropping below 12 credits/semester may jeopardize visa status. Always check with the Office of Global Services BEFORE you submit a withdrawal.
